description: TypeScript workflow authoring API, object and builder definitions, deterministic canonical IR, registry resolution, plugin registration, and structured validation diagnostics.

userStories.title: Implement public workflow authoring API surface
userStories.description: As a TypeScript workflow author, I want stable helpers so that I can define and validate workflows in code.
acceptanceCriteria: Export setupWorkflow, workflow, defineWorkflow, provideWorkflow, text, json, template, fixedProfile, validateWorkflow, compileWorkflow, serializeWorkflowForDebug, projectToXState, and resolveWorkflowDefinition or style-equivalent names
Export registerWorkflow, registerWorkflowHandler, registerWorkflowAdapter, registerWorkflowGuard, registerWorkflowHumanAction, and registerPluginWorkflows or style-equivalent registration helpers
API does not expose runtime-kernel internals as authoring primitives
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Normalize builder and object definitions to equivalent IR
userStories.description: As an agent editing workflow code, I want object definitions and builder definitions to produce the same normalized shape so that either style is safe.
acceptanceCriteria: workflow(id) builder supports a one-node Agent workflow in under 15 lines
defineWorkflow(id, definition) object style supports the same workflow semantics
Equivalent object and builder definitions serialize to equivalent deterministic canonical IR in snapshot tests
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scope setupWorkflow without persisting closures
userStories.description: As a framework developer, I want setupWorkflow to scope dependencies by id so that run records remain serializable and auditable.
acceptanceCriteria: setupWorkflow scopes types, profiles, handlers, guards, adapters, prompt assets, human actions, and capability metadata
Persisted workflow definitions and run records store ids/refs instead of inline closures
provideWorkflow can override implementations for tests/deployments while preserving id/version/hash auditability
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Workflow Registry reference resolution
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want registry hooks so that plugins can publish workflows and implementation refs.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow Registry registers and resolves workflow definitions, code handlers, adapters, guards, prompt builders/assets, human actions, routing hints, capability declarations, and custom merge handlers
registerPluginWorkflows(pluginId, register) can add at least one workflow, handler, adapter, guard, prompt asset, and human action
Runtime starts workflows from registry-resolved workflow id and version
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Reject missing or invalid registry references during validation
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I want invalid references rejected before execution so that workflows fail early with actionable errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Validation rejects unknown workflow ids, handler ids, adapter ids, guard ids, prompt assets, human actions, and statically resolvable profile/tool/skill refs
Diagnostics include code, severity, path or node/edge id when applicable, and fix hint for top V1 authoring failures
Negative tests cover missing ids and unsupported definitions
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add deterministic debug serialization metadata
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I want deterministic serialization so that workflow definitions can be snapshot-tested.
acceptanceCriteria: serializeWorkflowForDebug outputs stable ordering for workflow metadata, nodes, edges, ports, and registry refs
IR metadata supports useWhen, notFor, examples, tags, routing hints, prompt asset refs, version, definition hash, and migration metadata when needed
Debug serialization is documented as not being a V1 workflow file import/export product surface
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add port utility types for workflow and node values
userStories.description: As a TypeScript workflow author, I want utility types tied to ports so that handler inputs and outputs are typed.
acceptanceCriteria: Expose utility types equivalent to WorkflowInputFrom, WorkflowOutputFrom, NodeInputFrom, NodeOutputFrom, and WorkflowSnapshotFrom
Utility types infer values from text and JSON ports without requiring XState types in authoring code
Type-level tests or compile fixtures validate expected inference
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
